danger_signals.visual_form: Red health bars on character portraits at screen bottom
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; enemies in combat range or engaged

danger_signals.visual_form: Icons displayed beneath character portraits (level drain, paralysis, etc.)
danger_signals.meaning: Character is debilitated or under negative effect; vulnerability state

danger_signals.visual_form: Red bar reduction on character portrait
danger_signals.meaning: Character approaching incapacitation; critical danger threshold

danger_signals.visual_form: Marshland environment; hovels and crude structures in Nulb
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger zone; evil forces concentrated in these areas

danger_signals.visual_form: Increasing encounter density in specific regions (bandits, kobolds, goblins)
danger_signals.meaning: World-state shift toward active threat; unsafe travel conditions

safety_signals.visual_form: Established settlement with NPCs; militia presence; chapel structures
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; cleared/controlled zone; organized resistance established

safety_signals.visual_form: Absence of red bars or status condition icons on character portraits
safety_signals.meaning: Party at full health and unimpaired; safe state

safety_signals.visual_form: Up to three NPC companions visible in party formation
safety_signals.meaning: Reinforcement available; faction support; reduced isolation risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Waldgraf's militia presence in Hommlet region
safety_signals.meaning: Authority control; patrolled territory; organized resistance to evil

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kobolds, goblins, bandits as foot soldiers; elite temple servants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Marshland settlements (Nulb); six miles from Hommlet; crude hovels; temple chapel

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Militia organization; established structures; chapel of good alignment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hommlet town center; organized militia presence; safe roads; established buildings

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Suspected source of evil influx; bandits and monsters from region
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Forestlands of Wild Coast; Dyvers of Nyr Dyv; external threat origin

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Pre-conflict prosperity (13 years before game start)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hommlet thriving; safe roads; low taxes; flourishing village infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs engaged in trade and peaceful activity; no combat encounters in town

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasing monster and bandit presence; emergence of Nulb settlement; marshland development
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies trickling into region; hostile encounters escalate; roads become unsafe

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Militia presence in Hommlet; visible defenses; enemy stronghold in Nulb six miles away
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat encounters frequent; militia engaged in ongoing defense; tension visible in NPC dialogue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Status condition icons (level drain, paralysis); red health bars; character portrait graying
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character unable to act or limited in action economy; requires healing intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All characters within one step of party alignment; visual consistency in character selection screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party morale maintained; NPC followers available; group actions synchronized

environmental_storytelling.example: Nulb settlement located exactly six miles from Hommlet; marshland isolation; physical distance communicates threat containment and frontier conflict
Emergence of Nulb from hovels; growth from small encampment to structured settlement; visual expansion indicates escalating evil presence
Hommlet's established buildings, chapel, and militia structure; organized layout vs. crude Nulb hovels; architecture communicates civilization level and control
Safe roads in prosperous era; roads becoming unsafe with evil influx; traversal risk communicated through encounter frequency and enemy density

environmental_storytelling.technique: Monster type as regional origin marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Specific monster types (kobolds, goblins, bandits) indicate Wild Coast/Nyr Dyv regional origin; creature variety signals external invasion

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full action economy; radial menu shows all five action types available; character portrait unmarked
Red health bar visible; reduced action capacity; status condition icons appear; movement speed potentially reduced
Character portrait grayed; unable to act; loses hit points per round; requires heal check to stabilize
Encounters increase in frequency; bandits, kobolds, goblins spawn in higher density; movement patterns more aggressive
Confined to Nulb marshlands; prevented from expanding toward Hommlet; militia presence limits movement
Appear in party formation; join radial menu selections; provide familiar/companion support; visible on character screen tabs
Present in Hommlet; checking evil forces; visible patrolling; maintaining territorial control

notes: The Temple of Elemental Evil uses a turn-based tactical system where visual state is communicated primarily through UI elements (health bars, status icons, radial menus) and environmental design. Danger and safety are signaled through geography (Nulb vs. Hommlet), encounter density, and character status indicators. Faction identity is expressed through settlement type, NPC affiliation, and environmental markers rather than explicit visual symbols. World-state shifts from prosperity to conflict are communicated through enemy presence escalation and environmental development. The game's alignment system (one-step coherence) provides additional faction/political cohesion signaling.
